name: Brown Rice Jambalaya
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Diced ham or bacon (cut-bacon crosswise into thin-strips)
1 medium Green bell pepper, cored-cut in 1-inch squares
1/2 tsp Each, Dried oregano leaves, Dried thyme leaves, File Powder, Ground black pepper
1/4 tsp Each, Cayenne pepper, Ground cumin
recipeInstructions:
1. Render fat from ham or bacon in a 4-quart soup kettle over low heat.
2. Increase heat to medium and stir until cooked, about 5 minutes.
3. Remove chicken skin, cut meat off the bones, and cut boneless chicken into bite-size pieces. Add to kettle or skillet with bacon or ham and toss until color turns pale, about 4 minutes. Remove bacon or ham and chicken with a slotted spoon and set aside.
4. Brown sausage in the kettle over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes, then remove. Leave 2 tablespoons fat in the kettle; discard remaining fat.
5. Mince garlic using a metal blade in a food processor.
6. Add onion and chop coarsely with half-second pulses.
7. Add green pepper and process until mixture is chopped to medium consistency.
8. Add onion and green pepper mixture to the kettle and stir over low heat until softened, about 10 minutes.
9. Process tomatoes until pureed; set aside.
10. Add rice to ingredients in the kettle and stir over low heat for 2 minutes. Stir in oregano, thyme, file powder, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and cumin.
11. Add tomatoes and broth to the mixture and stir well. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook rice mixture for 15 to 20 minutes or until rice is tender (rice may not absorb all liquid).
12. Remove sausage and add diced pieces into the rice; cover and cook for an additional 20 minutes.
13. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding salt if necessary.
14. Stir shrimp into hot rice mixture and serve with shrimp, meats, and liquid.
Serves 8.
